artifact.content_preview:
From: dave.chen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: carol.nguyen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration
Date: March 7, 2026

Alice, Carol,

While reviewing the network migration plan for Site A, I wanted to flag
an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

Given that our contractors will already be on-site and the network
infrastructure will be partially disrupted during the Catalyst 9500
switch deployme

extracted_tasks.text: Discuss Wi-Fi infrastructure upgrade opportunity with the steering committee
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I wanted to flag an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

extracted_tasks.text: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points from Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Upgrading to the Catalyst 9120 series during the same migration window would avoid a separate disruptive maintenance activity later in the year

extracted_tasks.text: Obtain steering committee approval for the expanded scope and budget
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Could we add this to the scope and get steering committee approval before the March 18 window?

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: Leverage existing on-site contractors and network disruption for Wi-Fi upgrade
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: this would be an ideal time to also upgrade the Wi-Fi access points across all three data centre facilities.

extracted_tasks.text: Procure Catalyst 9120 access points via TechSupplier
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I've already spoken informally with TechSupplier and they can include the access points in the same delivery.

metadata.subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration

alerts.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of timeline impact.
alerts.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — the approved network hardware scope is limited to Catalyst 9500 switches only; OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative'
alerts.suggested_action: Do not proceed informally. The Project Manager must log this as a formal Change Request under the SSC Project Change Management Process.
```json
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A",
    "AD schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Steering committee presentations (monthly)",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments",
    "Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "D-001: Project Management Plan — Due: October 31, 2024",
    "D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due: December 15, 2024",
    "D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due: January 31, 2025",
    "D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due: February 28, 2025",
    "D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due: March 10, 2026",
    "D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due: April 30, 2026",
    "D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due: May 31, 2026",
    "D-008: Project Closure Report — Due: June 30, 2026"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes",
    "Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure",
    "Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board",
    "Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)",
    "Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval",
    "All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor",
    "Compliance required with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach",
    "Compliance required with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "Compliance required with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "Compliance required with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "Compliance required with the Official Languages Act for all client-facing deliverables"
  ]
}
```

in_scope: Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure
Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A
Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
BGP routing table migration and validation
Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due: October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due: December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due: January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due: February 28, 2025
D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due: March 10, 2026
D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due: April 30, 2026
D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due: May 31, 2026
D-008: Project Closure Report — Due: June 30, 2026
constraints: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes
Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure
Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board
Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026
Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)
Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval
All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor
Compliance required with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach
Compliance required with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy
Compliance required with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security
Compliance required with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)
Compliance required with the Official Languages Act for all client-facing deliverables

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.15,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of timeline impact.",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — the approved network hardware scope is limited to Catalyst 9500 switches only; OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative' — establishes the precedent that ancillary network infrastructure upgrades are explicitly deferred; CONSTRAINT: 'Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes' and 'All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor'",
  "suggested_action": "Do not proceed informally. The Project Manager must log this as a formal Change Request under the SSC Project Change Management Process. The CR must quantify the full budget impact ($85,000–$110,000 against the approved $2,400,000 CAD ceiling), assess whether the addition breaches the 15% variance threshold triggering Treasury Board submission, evaluate the 9-day schedule impact against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint (D-006), and obtain written approval from the Project Sponsor before any procurement or vendor discussions are advanced. Dave Chen's informal engagement with TechSupplier must be flagged as a procurement governance risk and halted pending formal approval.",
  "reasoning": "The request to upgrade Wi-Fi access points is a clearly additive scope element with no basis in the approved scope baseline. The baseline authorises Catalyst 9500 switch deployment specifically; wireless access point infrastructure is a distinct technology layer not referenced anywhere in the in-scope items. The estimated cost of $85,000–$110,000 represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase on the approved $2,400,000 envelope, which — while below the 15% Treasury Board threshold on its own — still requires a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure per the stated constraints. The 9-day timeline addition also carries material risk to the April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline (D-006) given the document is dated March 7, 2026, leaving fewer than 8 weeks to Go-Live. The operational efficiency rationale (avoiding a separate maintenance window, EOL hardware) is legitimate and may support approval, but it does not bypass the mandatory change governance process. The informal vendor engagement by Dave Chen is a secondary concern that may implicate GC procurement rules and should be addressed separately. The alignment score is set low (0.15) because while the work is physically adjacent to in-scope activities, it is substantively out of scope, budget-impacting, schedule-impacting, and was initiated outside the required change management process."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of timeline impact.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — the approved network hardware scope is limited to Catalyst 9500 switches only; OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative' — establishes the precedent that ancillary network infrastructure upgrades are explicitly deferred; CONSTRAINT: 'Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes' and 'All scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process and require written approval from the Project Sponsor'
parsed_json.suggested_action: Do not proceed informally. The Project Manager must log this as a formal Change Request under the SSC Project Change Management Process. The CR must quantify the full budget impact ($85,000–$110,000 against the approved $2,400,000 CAD ceiling), assess whether the addition breaches the 15% variance threshold triggering Treasury Board submission, evaluate the 9-day schedule impact against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint (D-006), and obtain written approval from the Project Sponsor before any procurement or vendor discussions are advanced. Dave Chen's informal engagement with TechSupplier must be flagged as a procurement governance risk and halted pending formal approval.
parsed_json.reasoning: The request to upgrade Wi-Fi access points is a clearly additive scope element with no basis in the approved scope baseline. The baseline authorises Catalyst 9500 switch deployment specifically; wireless access point infrastructure is a distinct technology layer not referenced anywhere in the in-scope items. The estimated cost of $85,000–$110,000 represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase on the approved $2,400,000 envelope, which — while below the 15% Treasury Board threshold on its own — still requires a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure per the stated constraints. The 9-day timeline addition also carries material risk to the April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline (D-006) given the document is dated March 7, 2026, leaving fewer than 8 weeks to Go-Live. The operational efficiency rationale (avoiding a separate maintenance window, EOL hardware) is legitimate and may support approval, but it does not bypass the mandatory change governance process. The informal vendor engagement by Dave Chen is a secondary concern that may implicate GC procurement rules and should be addressed separately. The alignment score is set low (0.15) because while the work is physically adjacent to in-scope activities, it is substantively out of scope, budget-impacting, schedule-impacting, and was initiated outside the required change management process.
